tasks.criterion: `run_in_sandbox` API shape and security defaults are correct (A7 behavioral): read `src/trine_eval/sandbox/docker.py`. The implementation must expose `run_in_sandbox` with `network` defaulting to `"none"`, always include `--rm`, `--network`, `--cpus`, `--memory`, and bind-mount in the docker argv; catch `TimeoutExpired` returning `SandboxResult(timed_out=True)` without re-raising; invoke a post-call `docker ps --filter label=...` check; and return `SandboxResult` with all five fields. A function that defaults `network` to anything other than `"none"`, omits `--rm`, or omits the post-call container check is FAIL.

Regression gate design is correct — PASS_TO_PASS auto-fail and `RegressionGateResult` shape (D4 behavioral): read `src/trine_eval/sandbox/regression_gate.py`. The implementation must expose `evaluate_regression_gate` with the required parameters; run the test suite BEFORE applying the patch; apply the patch; run AFTER; detect PASS->FAIL transitions in `pass_to_pass` and add to `pass_to_pass_regressions`; set `verdict="fail"` if regressions exist OR any `fail_to_pass` test still fails; return `RegressionGateResult` with `fail_to_pass_passed`, `fail_to_pass_total`, `pass_to_pass_regressions`, and `verdict`. An unconditional `verdict="pass"` return or missing pre-patch baseline is FAIL.

Historical Phase 1 and Phase 2 prior-sprint contracts and evals are unmodified: no file matching Phase-1 or prior Phase-2 sprint paths is modified by any commit whose message contains `(sprint-05)`. Verification iterates over all `(sprint-05)`-prefixed commits reachable from HEAD but not from the sprint-04 Phase-2 completion commit and checks each commit's individual diff against the prior-sprint glob. The Phase-2 patterns use `sprint-0[1234]*.md` (covering sprint-01 through sprint-04). When no sprint-05 commits exist, the loop exits PASS.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'failed=0; S04BASE=$(git log --format=%H --grep="complete sprint 04 (Phase 2)" -1 2>/dev/null); SPRINT_COMMITS=$(git log --format=%H --grep="(sprint-05)" HEAD ${S04BASE:+^$S04BASE} 2>/dev/null); for c in $SPRINT_COMMITS; do diff_out=$(git diff "${c}^".."${c}" -- ".harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-9]*.md" ".harness/contracts/sprint-1*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-0[1-9]*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-1*.md" ".harness/contracts/phase-02/sprint-0[1234]*.md" ".harness/contracts/phase-02/sprint-1*.md" ".harness/evals/phase-02/sprint-0[1234]*.md" ".harness/evals/phase-02/sprint-1*.md" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$diff_out" ]; then echo "FAIL: $c modified prior-sprint files"; echo "$diff_out" | head -20; failed=1; fi; done; [ "$failed" = "0" ] && echo PASS || exit 1'

tasks.criterion: No forbidden packages imported or declared: the strings `langgraph`, `ragas`, `pgvector`, and `fastapi` must not appear in `pyproject.toml`, `src/trine_eval/**/*.py`, or `tests/**/*.py`.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'git grep -rn --count -E "langgraph|ragas|pgvector|fastapi" pyproject.toml src/ tests/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^Binary" | grep -v ":0$" && echo FAIL && exit 1 || echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: No real `docker run` invocations in tests — every `subprocess.run` call in `tests/sandbox/` is mocked: the Evaluator reads `tests/sandbox/*.py` and confirms that every call that would invoke `subprocess.run([..."docker"...])` is enclosed in a `unittest.mock.patch("subprocess.run", ...)` context manager or `mocker.patch(...)`. A bare `subprocess.run` call without a surrounding patch is FAIL. No actual `docker run` command is executed during `uv run pytest tests/sandbox/`.
